requested.mainContext:
You represent ONE WARE GmbH.

Core positioning:
- ONE WARE sells software that automatically creates production-ready Physical AI, Vision AI, and Edge AI models in minutes instead of months.
- The core business value is less trial and error, faster delivery, more predictable project timelines, smaller and more efficient models, lower development costs, local training options, open API access, and vendor-independent deployment.
- ONE WARE is strongest when messaging stays close to concrete delivery problems, production constraints, integration friction, cost-to-serve, and hardware realities instead of generic AI hype.
- Keep the value proposition grounded in production readiness: fast path from data to usable model, repeatable deployment, lower engineering overhead, and a practical route to customer value.

Messaging rules:
- Do not rewrite outreach from scratch every time.
- Start from the segment template and personalize only where there is a clear factual hook.
- Keep ONE WARE's USP visible: less trial and error, faster delivery, more predictable projects, local training, smaller hardware-efficient models, lower development effort.
- Personalization should point to a concrete delivery bottleneck, use case, or market signal, not generic flattery.
requested.searchStrategyContext:
You are steering a Germany-first campaign for ONE WARE focused on software service providers and system integrators.

Priority for this run:
- Find German software integrators, automation integrators, embedded/industrial software service providers, and technical project-delivery firms.
- Strongest fit is explicit Vision AI, Industrial AI, image processing, industrial automation, inspection, surveillance, medtech imaging, or similar project delivery.
- Also accept firms with broader AI messaging only when they clearly deliver customer projects and a future Vision AI / Industrial AI path is plausible.
- Prefer service-heavy, project-led, implementation-oriented firms.

Avoid for this run:
- Media, publishers, magazines, editorial brands, event businesses, associations, universities, research institutes.
- Product-led robotics makers, OEM hardware vendors, and companies whose main business is selling robots or hardware products.
- Own AI platform vendors or software products that make ONE WARE look like a direct competitor.
- Pure consultancies, strategy firms, recruiting, finance, investors, resellers, distributors, and generic SaaS without delivery ownership.
- Non-German companies unless an unusually strong German delivery footprint is visible.

prequalification.mainContext: Classify completely unbiased first. First determine the company archetype. Media, publisher, magazine, event, university, research institute, investor, bank, insurer, recruiting, reseller, generic advisory, and product-led robotics or OEM hardware profiles must not land in integrator categories. Positive categories require actual delivery ownership and industrial or vision-relevant implementation signals. If no verified company description is available, default to other or irrelevant unless there is strong explicit evidence.

researchPriorities: Look for fixed-price pressure and repeatable project patterns.
outreachPriorities: Lead with throughput and margin improvements.
Keep partner framing and project efficiency language.
personalizationRules: Personalize only with concrete delivery hooks.
Retain template backbone and ONE WARE USP wording.
avoidSignals: Generic AI buzzwords without delivery context

researchPriorities: Identify where generic AI delivery can benefit from faster vision model cycles.
Check if they implement projects, not only advise.
outreachPriorities: Connect ONE WARE to project speed and predictability.
personalizationRules: Personalize around current delivery workflow gaps.

researchPriorities: Pinpoint vertical-specific delivery bottlenecks and hardware constraints.
outreachPriorities: Anchor message in vertical project risk reduction.

researchPriorities: Confirm production scale and internal engineering capability.
Find QC, inspection, and process automation leverage points.
outreachPriorities: Lead with economics, feasibility, and speed to production.

researchPriorities: Validate partner fit and customer AI-enablement potential.
outreachPriorities: Focus on enabling customer-ready AI setups.
avoidSignals: Positioning as replacement of core hardware business

researchPriorities: Validate OEM integration pathways and customer-facing AI option potential.
outreachPriorities: Position ONE WARE as fast AI enablement layer for machine offerings.
personalizationRules: Use machine/product context, not generic AI language.

outreachPriorities: Lead with integration leverage and user value lift.
personalizationRules: Tie outreach to concrete platform workflows.

suggestedFilters.name: DACH General AI Integrators Refined
suggestedFilters.persona: Software or consulting integrator with general AI focus and real implementation ownership
suggestedFilters.notes: Use when AI capability is visible, even if vision specialization is not explicit yet. Market focus: DE.

suggestedFilters.persona: Software integrator with explicit machine-vision or industrial-AI delivery ownership
suggestedFilters.notes: Primary ICP. Focus on delivery-oriented teams with recurring vision-heavy projects and explicit machine vision or industrial AI delivery. Market focus: DE.

suggestedFilters.persona: Integrator active in relevant verticals like surveillance, defence, medtech, robotics, drones, agriculture, automotive
suggestedFilters.notes: Focus on vertical specialists with recurring vision and edge AI delivery bottlenecks. Prioritize Germany and strong European industrial hubs.

evaluations.recommendation: Low signal. Replace or significantly revise the filter. Early stop triggered after 11 reviews.

researchBriefs.linkedInMessage: Kurze Frage: Wie schnell bekommen Sie aktuell ein Vision-AI-Modell produktionsbereit? Ich sehe bei Integratoren oft, dass Modellwahl und Optimierung unnötig viel Zeit kosten. Wir automatisieren genau diesen Teil. Wie ist das bei Ihnen aktuell?
researchBriefs.emailBody:
Hallo Herr/Frau [Name],

Arbeiten Sie aktuell an Vision-AI-Projekten, bei denen es viele Iterationen braucht, bis ein Modell einsatzbereit ist – oder es trotz mehrerer Versuche nicht zuverlässig funktioniert?

Genau das sehen wir häufig bei Integratoren: Wochen bis Monate fließen in Tuning und Deployment, statt in die eigentliche Lösung.

Wir haben dafür eine Software entwickelt, mit der sich individuelle Vision-AI-Modelle in unter 5 Minuten erzeugen und direkt produktionsbereit einsetzen lassen. Dadurch können Teams deutlich schneller iterieren, Projektlaufzeiten besser planen und mit dem gleichen Team mehr Projekte umsetzen.

Mich würde interessieren: Wo liegt bei Ihnen aktuell der größte Engpass in Vision-AI-Projekten – eher in der Modellgenerierung oder in der Integration?

Mit freundlichen Grüßen
[Ihr Name]
researchBriefs.phoneScript: Hallo Herr/Frau [Name], [Ihr Name] von ONE WARE hier. Ich mache es kurz – passt es gerade für 30 Sekunden? Wir sprechen aktuell mit Integratoren, bei denen Modellwahl und Optimierung in Vision-AI-Projekten Wochen oder Monate kosten. Wir automatisieren genau diesen Teil. Wie ist das bei Ihnen aktuell?
researchBriefs.eventIdea: Check for presence at SPS, Automatica, Vision, or regional automation events.

researchBriefs.overview: OCTUM GmbH is a German industrial machine vision integrator specializing in customized AOI and inline inspection systems for regulated and high-volume industries such as pharmaceuticals, medical technology, packaging, and automotive. They provide end-to-end project delivery from feasibility to commissioning and lifecycle service, focusing on 100% optical quality control and compliance with standards like ISO 9001 and GMP. OCTUM operates as a technology-open, manufacturer-independent integrator primarily serving German production environments.
researchBriefs.qualificationSummary: OCTUM is a strong fit as a Vision AI integrator with recurring delivery ownership across project lifecycles and fixed-price pressures typical in regulated industrial sectors. Their focus on inline AOI and optical quality assurance aligns well with ONE WARE's value proposition of reducing trial and error, accelerating model production, and enabling predictable project timelines. The company’s emphasis on compliance and lifecycle support suggests a need for reliable, repeatable AI model deployment and integration efficiency.
qualifyingSignals: Customized AOI and inline inspection system integrator with full project lifecycle ownership
Focus on regulated industries requiring high reliability and compliance (pharma, medical, automotive)
Manufacturer-independent and technology-open approach indicating flexibility in AI model integration
Emphasis on 100% optical quality control and traceability in mass production
ISO 9001 certification and compliance with GMP/21 CFR Part 11 standards
Service and remanufacturing offerings indicating long-term system ownership and recurring projects
riskFlags: No evidence of proprietary hardware manufacturing; primarily an integrator using third-party technologies
Limited public evidence of recent commercial momentum or large-scale expansion
No explicit public mention of AI model development bottlenecks or current AI tooling challenges
researchBriefs.businessPotentialReasoning: Typical single Vision AI use cases start around 7,000 EUR, with complex production-grade AOI systems in regulated industries often reaching 20,000 to 40,000 EUR per AI model. Given OCTUM’s recurring project lifecycle involvement and multiple industry verticals, initial engagements could be in this range with potential to scale across multiple projects and clients.
researchBriefs.targetIndustry: Industrial Machine Vision, Pharmaceuticals, Medical Technology, Packaging, Automotive
researchBriefs.productsOffered: Customized AOI systems, Inline inspection systems, Optical quality assurance solutions, Lifecycle service and remanufacturing
researchBriefs.personalizationRule: Personalize only with concrete delivery bottlenecks or integration challenges related to Vision AI model iteration speed, reliability, or project timeline predictability.
researchBriefs.linkedInAngle: Kurze Frage: Wie schnell bekommen Sie aktuell ein Vision-AI-Modell produktionsbereit? Ich sehe bei Integratoren oft, dass Modellwahl und Optimierung unnötig viel Zeit kosten. Wir automatisieren genau diesen Teil. Wie ist das bei Ihnen aktuell?
researchBriefs.emailAngle: Fokussieren Sie auf Engpässe bei der Modellgenerierung oder Integration in Ihren Vision-AI-Projekten, um Projektlaufzeiten zu verkürzen und die Effizienz zu steigern.
researchBriefs.phoneAngle: Kurz anfragen, ob Optimierung und Deployment von Vision-AI-Modellen bei OCTUM aktuell viel Zeit beanspruchen und ob Interesse an Automatisierung besteht.
researchBriefs.linkedInMessage: Kurze Frage: Wie schnell bekommen Sie aktuell ein Vision-AI-Modell produktionsbereit? Ich sehe bei Integratoren oft, dass Modellwahl und Optimierung unnötig viel Zeit kosten. Wir automatisieren genau diesen Teil. Wie ist das bei Ihnen aktuell?

researchBriefs.overview: Muckenhirn Automation GmbH is a German vendor-independent industrial automation and software engineering company focusing on robotics programming, PLC software, MES, and Industry 4.0 solutions. While they have strong automation and MES capabilities, there is no clear public evidence of a dedicated machine vision or industrial AI specialization. Their delivery model appears to be custom automation software and on-site commissioning across multiple industries in Germany.
researchBriefs.qualificationSummary: Muckenhirn Automation fits the profile of an industrial automation integrator with recurring delivery ownership and fixed-price project pressure. However, there is no explicit public signal that they currently own or deliver vision-heavy AI projects, which is ONE WARE's core USP focus. Their vendor-independent approach and MES/digitalization experience suggest potential openness to AI model automation to improve throughput and margin, but this remains speculative without concrete vision AI project evidence.
qualifyingSignals: Vendor-independent industrial automation and software engineering
Recurring delivery of robotics programming and PLC software projects
MES and Industry 4.0 digitalization expertise indicating production environment integration
Custom automation software and on-site commissioning implying fixed-price and repeatable project patterns
riskFlags: No public evidence of machine vision or industrial AI specialization
Category fit is broader industrial automation rather than focused Vision AI
Small private company with limited third-party coverage
Potential mismatch between ONE WARE's Vision AI automation USP and Muckenhirn's current public profile
researchBriefs.businessPotentialReasoning: As a vendor-independent integrator with recurring automation projects, Muckenhirn could realistically start with a single AI use case around 7,000 EUR and scale to 20,000-40,000 EUR for production-grade Vision AI deployments integrated into their MES or robotics solutions. Without clear vision AI specialization, initial potential is moderate but could grow with successful pilot projects.
researchBriefs.productsOffered: Robotics programming, PLC software, MES/digitalization solutions, Equipment Automation Framework
researchBriefs.personalizationRule: Personalize only if a concrete delivery bottleneck in Vision AI model generation or integration is identified; otherwise keep the base template with emphasis on throughput and margin improvements in automation projects.

researchBriefs.overview: elunic AG is a Munich-based German industrial digitalization and AI automation company with over 15 years of experience. They provide AI-driven Industry 4.0 software solutions including AI.SEE for automated visual quality inspection and eluna Multi Agent for automating industrial knowledge work. Their offerings emphasize data sovereignty, flexible deployment models (SaaS to on-premises), and open-source AI platforms. They focus on industrial use cases such as quality control, predictive maintenance, and service optimization.
researchBriefs.qualificationSummary: elunic AG is an experienced industrial AI integrator and software provider actively implementing AI automation projects, including vision AI for quality inspection. Their AI.SEE product uses neural networks combined with industrial image processing, indicating hands-on delivery rather than pure consulting. They address typical industrial production constraints and emphasize scalable automation, aligning well with ONE WARE's value proposition of faster, production-ready vision AI models with less trial and error. Their focus on data sovereignty and local deployment complements ONE WARE's local training and vendor-independent deployment strengths.
qualifyingSignals: Over 15 years of industrial digitalization and AI automation experience
Active delivery of AI projects including vision AI for quality inspection (AI.SEE)
Focus on industrial production use cases like weld seam inspection and coating inspection
Flexible deployment from SaaS to on-premises with data sovereignty
Use of neural networks combined with industrial image processing
Emphasis on scalable automation and repeatable delivery
Target industries include automotive, electronics, and medical technology
riskFlags: Primarily an integrator and software services company rather than a pure product vendor
Focus on industrial AI/IIoT and knowledge automation may limit fit for non-industrial or consumer-facing AI use cases
Recent rebranding and product naming changes may require clarification during outreach
researchBriefs.businessPotentialReasoning: As an industrial AI integrator with ongoing vision AI projects, elunic AG could realistically engage in multiple AI use cases starting around 7,000 EUR each. Given their scale and focus on production-grade deployments, a typical project could range between 20,000 to 40,000 EUR. Their industrial customer base and scalable automation approach suggest potential for repeat business and multiple deployments, supporting a mid-range business potential estimate.
researchBriefs.targetIndustry: Industrial Automation, Manufacturing, Quality Control, Automotive, Electronics, Medical Technology
researchBriefs.productsOffered: AI.SEE (automated visual quality inspection), eluna Multi Agent (industrial AI automation platform), Industry 4.0 software solutions
researchBriefs.personalizationRule: Personalize around elunic's current delivery workflow gaps in vision AI model optimization and deployment speed, referencing their AI.SEE product and industrial inspection use cases to highlight how ONE WARE can reduce trial and error and accelerate production-ready model delivery.
researchBriefs.linkedInAngle: Ask about their biggest bottleneck in vision AI projects—model generation or integration—and offer ONE WARE's automation to speed up production-ready model creation tailored for industrial hardware.
researchBriefs.emailAngle: Focus on the common delivery challenge of prolonged model selection and optimization phases in vision AI projects like AI.SEE, proposing ONE WARE's software to automate and accelerate these steps for more predictable project timelines and efficient resource use.
researchBriefs.phoneAngle: Directly address the time-consuming aspects of vision AI model development and deployment in industrial projects, positioning ONE WARE as a solution to automate and speed up these phases, improving delivery predictability and reducing engineering overhead.
researchBriefs.linkedInMessage: Kurze Frage: Wo liegt bei elunic AG aktuell der größte Engpass bei Vision-AI-Projekten – eher in der Modellgenerierung oder in der Integration? Genau diesen Schritt automatisieren wir, um Produktionsmodelle schneller und effizienter bereitzustellen.
researchBriefs.emailSubject: AI-Projekte schneller zu produktionsreifer Vision-AI machen
researchBriefs.emailBody:
Hallo Herr/Frau [Name],

Viele Teams mit allgemeinem AI-Fokus sehen in Vision-Projekten denselben Engpass: Modellwahl, Optimierung und deploymentnahe Anpassungen ziehen sich oft länger als geplant.

ONE WARE automatisiert genau diesen Teil. Aus Ihren Daten entstehen in Minuten produktionsreife Vision-AI-Modelle, optimiert für die Zielhardware.

So lassen sich mehr Kundenprojekte mit dem gleichen Team liefern, bei besser planbaren Laufzeiten.

Wäre ein kurzer Austausch sinnvoll, um zu prüfen, ob das für Ihre laufenden Delivery-Projekte relevant ist?

Mit freundlichen Grüßen
[Ihr Name]
researchBriefs.phoneScript: Hallo Herr/Frau [Name], [Ihr Name] von ONE WARE. Wir sprechen mit AI-Dienstleistern, bei denen Vision-Projekte in Modellwahl und Optimierung zu viel Zeit kosten. Genau das automatisieren wir. Ist das bei elunic AG ein Thema?
researchBriefs.eventIdea: Organize a focused workshop or webinar on accelerating industrial vision AI delivery, showcasing how ONE WARE's automated model generation can complement elunic's AI.SEE and industrial automation projects to reduce time-to-production and engineering effort.
